sizing_rule.note: Use fractional Kelly (half f or smaller) to reduce maximum drawdown by ~50% and smooth equity curves. Position Size = (fraction of f) × (Account Equity / Largest Historical Loss). Trade-off: slower capital accumulation for reduced variance.

source_rule_text: Use a fraction of optimal f (half f or smaller) to reduce drawdown and variance, accepting longer time to reach goals in exchange for smoother equity curves.
